incidence.value: high (episodes common); recurrent cases increasing with diabetes and antibiotic use

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Typical symptoms (pruritus, discharge) plus microscopy (budding yeast/pseudohyphae) when available; pH usually normal; culture for recurrent/complicated cases

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: High for uncomplicated cases; recurrent VVC requires longer regimens

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Often clinically diagnosed in APS; microscopy/culture availability varies
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Topical azoles or oral fluconazole; longer maintenance regimens for recurrent VVC; address diabetes control and triggers
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Misdiagnosis vs bacterial vaginosis/STIs and limited lab confirmation for recurrent cases
